# pfsense_gateway_groups (Data Source)

Retrieves all [gateway groups](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/routing/gateway-groups.html). Gateway groups organize multiple gateways into tiers for failover and load balancing.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_gateway_groups" "all" {}

output "gateway_group_names" {
  value = [for g in data.pfsense_gateway_groups.all.gateway_groups : g.name]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `gateway_groups` (Attributes List) List of gateway groups. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_groups))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_groups`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).
- `keep_failover_states` (String) Control whether firewall states are kept or killed when a gateway recovers from failure. Empty string uses global behavior.
- `members` (Attributes List) List of gateway members with their priority tiers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_groups--members))
- `name` (String) Name of the gateway group. Must be unique and cannot conflict with gateway names.
- `trigger` (String) Trigger level that determines when a member gateway is excluded from the group. Options: `down`, `downloss`, `downlatency`, `downlosslatency`.

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_groups--members"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_groups.members`

Read-Only:

- `gateway` (String) Name of the gateway to include in this group.
- `tier` (Number) Priority tier for this gateway (1-5). Gateways in the same tier are load balanced; lower tiers are preferred for failover.
- `virtual_ip` (String) Virtual IP address to use for this gateway member. Leave empty to use the gateway's interface address.
